rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Motion And Renewed Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs (Code Civ. Proc. Section 1008(B))
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 19, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC NOTICE OF RENEWED MOTION AND RENEWED MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS (CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 1008(B)) is continued to September 19, 2024 for parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion no later than September 3, 2024 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (Reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VF1-NA TRUST VS. ENCARNACION G. SANDOVAL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 19, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MODESTO ROBLES DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action under CCP 1161a. Whether the procedures and protections applicable to tenants apply in this case is a question of fact that can only be addressed upon a determination of the moving party's (who is not an original named defendant in the complaint) status as a tenant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AARON ZHAN VS. MAY LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 19, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT MAY LEE, BRANDON KOSMERL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective. Without any argument, explanation or authority provided in opposition Plaintiff limited the notice regarding tenant's rights under SFRO 37.9(j) to "school children-tenant protective status" omitting the information regarding protection and its potential forfeiture afforded to educators. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. MICHAEL ROGERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion. Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment; Proposed Response; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 19, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL ROGERS NOTICE OF MOTION, MOTION. MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND JUDGMENT; PROPOSED RESPONSE; PROPOSED ORDER is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1010.6 and Defendant failed to comply with the Order Shortening Time requiring that the motion to vacate be filed and served on August 12, 2024. The Motion was not filed until 1:39 p.m. on August 13, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. LOMBARD FLATS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 19, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT LOMBARD FLATS, LLC MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED. The issues of possession is in dispute in this action and it has not been "ascertained" by the Court that possession has been returned to the Plaintiff at this time. Additionally, this motion is not set on sufficient notice having been filed and served by mail on August 7, 2024 for August 19, 2024 hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
